seo.imageAlt: A freshly baked loaf of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, golden brown with creamy filling and garlic butter glaze.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the viral sensation! This homemade Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is soft, sweet, savory, and utterly irresistible.

description: Experience the viral sensation of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread! This irresistible treat features soft, buttery bread generously filled with a sweet, tangy cream cheese mixture and drenched in a savory garlic butter glaze, creating a perfect harmony of flavors and textures. It's a popular street food snack that's surprisingly easy to recreate at home for a truly delightful culinary adventure.

ingredients.notes: soft, slightly sweet buns work best
large, for the garlic butter mixture
for garnish, plus extra for mixture

instructions.title: Prepare the Bread Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Take each round bread bun and make 6 deep cuts from the top, without cutting all the way through the bottom. The cuts should radiate outwards from the center, creating 6 wedges that are still connected at the base.

instructions.title: Make the Cream Cheese Filling
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, granulated sugar, and 2 tablespoons of heavy cream or milk. Mix thoroughly with a spatula or hand mixer until the mixture is smooth, creamy, and lump-free. Transfer this filling into a piping bag for easier application.

instructions.title: Fill the Bread Buns
instructions.text: Carefully open up the cuts in each bread bun and pipe the cream cheese filling generously into each crevice. Ensure the filling reaches deep into the cuts for a delicious burst of flavor in every bite. Set the filled buns aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Garlic Butter Glaze
instructions.text: In another bowl, whisk together the melted unsalted butter, finely minced garlic, the large egg, 2 tablespoons of milk, and honey or maple syrup. Stir in about 1 teaspoon of dried parsley flakes. Mix well until all ingredients are fully combined.

instructions.title: Drench and Bake
instructions.text: Dip each filled bread bun completely into the garlic butter glaze, ensuring all surfaces and crevices are thoroughly coated. Alternatively, you can generously spoon or brush the glaze over the buns. Place the drenched buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.

instructions.title: Final Bake
instructions.text: Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown, the filling is slightly puffed, and the garlic butter is fragrant and bubbling. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread from the oven. Immediately sprinkle with extra dried parsley flakes for a fresh garnish. Serve warm and enjoy this sweet, savory, and utterly delicious Korean treat!

tips: For extra aroma and flavor, lightly toast the minced garlic in a bit of butter before adding it to the garlic butter glaze.
If you don't have a piping bag, you can use a ziplock bag with a corner snipped off, or simply use a spoon to carefully fill the bread crevices.
This bread is best enjoyed warm and fresh. If you have leftovers, reheat them briefly in a toaster oven or microwave until warm and soft again.
article:
Unlock a world of irresistible flavor with a dish that has taken the internet by storm and captivated palates worldwide: Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread. This viral sensation, hailing from the bustling bakeries and street food stalls of South Korea, offers a unique blend of sweet, savory, and utterly delicious. Forget everything you thought you knew about garlic bread; this Korean iteration elevates it to an art form, combining fluffy bread with a rich, tangy cream cheese filling and a fragrant, buttery garlic glaze. If you're ready to dive into the delightful realm of Korean baking, prepare to fall in love with this iconic treat.

## What is Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread?

At its heart, Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is a sophisticated take on a classic comfort food. Unlike its Western counterpart, which often features a simple garlic butter spread, the Korean version is a multi-layered masterpiece. It typically starts with a soft, round bread roll, often a brioche-like bun, that is deeply cut into six or eight wedges, but not all the way through, creating a flower-like appearance. These cuts are then generously filled with a sweet and tangy cream cheese mixture. The entire bread is then dipped into, or lavishly brushed with, a savory garlic butter sauce, which typically includes garlic, melted butter, eggs, sugar, and sometimes mayonnaise or condensed milk for an extra layer of richness and slight sweetness. Finally, it's baked until golden brown, resulting in an exterior that's crispy and aromatic, and an interior that's soft, creamy, and bursting with flavor. The combination of sweet cream cheese, savory garlic butter, and fluffy bread creates an unforgettable textural and taste experience that has made Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread a global phenomenon.

## Ingredients for Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

To embark on your journey of making this delectable treat, gather the following ingredients. Quality ingredients are key to achieving that authentic bakery taste.

For the Bread:
*   6 soft round bread rolls (brioche buns, milk buns, or even larger dinner rolls work best)

For the Cream Cheese Filling:
*   8 oz (226g) cream cheese, softened
*   2-3 tablespoons granulated sugar (adjust to your sweetness preference)
*   1 tablespoon milk or heavy cream
*   1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract (optional, but enhances flavor)

For the Garlic Butter Dip:
*   1/2 cup (113g) unsalted butter, melted
*   4-6 cloves garlic, minced very finely
*   1 large egg
*   2-3 tablespoons milk or heavy cream
*   1 tablespoon granulated sugar
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1-2 tablespoons mayonnaise (optional, adds richness and gloss)
*   Fresh parsley, chopped (for garnish, optional)

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Follow these steps carefully to recreate the magic of Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread in your own kitchen.

1.  **Prepare the Cream Cheese Filling:** In a medium bowl, combine the softened cream cheese, sugar, milk/cream, and vanilla extract (if using). Mix until smooth and creamy with no lumps. Transfer the filling into a piping bag or a Ziploc bag with a corner snipped off for easier application. Set aside.

2.  **Prepare the Garlic Butter Dip:** In another bowl, whisk together the melted butter, minced garlic, egg, milk/cream, sugar, salt, and mayonnaise (if using). Ensure all ingredients are well combined. The mixture should be pourable. Set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Bread:** Take each soft bread roll and make 6-8 deep cuts, starting from the top center and radiating outwards towards the edges, but do not cut all the way through the bottom. You want the cuts to create pockets for the filling.

4.  **Fill the Bread:** Using your piping bag, generously pipe the cream cheese filling into each of the cuts you made in the bread. You can also pipe a small dollop on top of the bread. Be generous, as the filling is a key component of the Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread experience.

5.  **Dip and Glaze:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Carefully pick up each filled bread roll and dip it entirely into the garlic butter mixture. Ensure all sides and the top are thoroughly coated. Alternatively, place the bread on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and generously brush the garlic butter over and into all the crevices.

6.  **Bake:** Place the coated bread rolls on the parchment-lined baking sheet. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the cream cheese filling is slightly bubbly. Keep an eye on it to prevent burning.

7.  **Garnish and Serve:** Once baked, remove from the oven. If desired, sprinkle with fresh chopped parsley for a pop of color and freshness. Serve immediately and enjoy your homemade Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread!

## Pro Tips for the Best Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread

Achieving bakery-quality results at home is easier with a few insider tricks:

*   **Choose the Right Bread:** The foundation is crucial. Opt for very soft, slightly sweet bread rolls like brioche buns or Japanese milk bread buns. Their texture perfectly complements the rich fillings.
*   **Soften Cream Cheese Properly:** Ensure your cream cheese is at room temperature for a smooth, lump-free filling. Cold cream cheese will result in a clumpy mixture that's hard to pipe.
*   **Don't Skimp on Garlic:** Freshly minced garlic is non-negotiable. Avoid pre-minced garlic in jars, as it lacks the pungent, fresh flavor needed for the garlic butter.
*   **Generous Cuts:** Make deep cuts in your bread to create ample pockets for the cream cheese filling. This ensures every bite is loaded with flavor.
*   **Flavor Balance:** Taste your cream cheese filling and garlic butter dip before assembly. Adjust sugar and salt to your preference. Some prefer a sweeter cream cheese, others a more savory garlic butter.
*   **Even Coating:** Ensure the bread is thoroughly coated in the garlic butter. This creates that delicious crispy, fragrant crust.
*   **Watch the Baking Time:** Ovens vary. Keep an eye on your bread during the last few minutes of baking to prevent it from drying out or burning.

## Serving Suggestions

Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is incredibly versatile. It shines brightest when served warm, fresh out of the oven, allowing the butter to melt and the cream cheese to remain soft and gooey. It makes for an excellent standalone snack, a unique appetizer for gatherings, or a delightful accompaniment to a light meal. Pair it with a cup of coffee or tea for an afternoon treat, or enjoy it alongside a simple salad or soup for a satisfying lunch. Its rich flavors also make it a fantastic addition to a brunch spread.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic recipe is divine, feel free to experiment:

*   **Spicy Kick:** Add a pinch of red pepper flakes to your garlic butter for a subtle heat.
*   **Herbal Notes:** Incorporate other fresh herbs like chives or oregano into the garlic butter for a different aromatic profile.
*   **Cheese Please:** For an extra cheesy experience, sprinkle some mozzarella or Parmesan cheese on top of the bread before baking.
*   **Vegan Option:** Use plant-based cream cheese and butter, and an egg substitute for a delicious vegan version. Look for soft vegan brioche buns.
*   **Different Breads:** While round buns are traditional, you can try slicing a baguette or ciabatta and applying the fillings and dip, though the experience will be slightly different.
*   **Sweetness Levels:** Adjust the amount of sugar in both the cream cheese and garlic butter to suit your palate. Some prefer a less sweet garlic butter.

## Storage Tips

Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread is best enjoyed fresh, but if you have leftovers, they can be stored.
*   **Room Temperature:** It can be left at room temperature in an airtight container for up to a day, though the bread may start to dry out slightly.
*   **Refrigeration:** For longer storage, refrigerate in an airtight container for up to 2-3 days. The texture of the bread and cream cheese will firm up when cold.
*   **Reheating:** To revive your Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread, reheat it in an oven or air fryer at 300°F (150°C) for 5-10 minutes, or until warmed through and slightly crispy. Avoid microwaving, as it can make the bread soggy.

Making Korean Cream Cheese Garlic Bread at home is a rewarding experience that brings a taste of authentic Korean baking right to your kitchen. With its harmonious blend of sweet, savory, and aromatic notes, it's more than just bread; it's a delightful culinary adventure. Give this recipe a try and discover why this viral sensation continues to win hearts around the globe.
